data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 end week with losses as AI trade tumbles led by Broadcom guidance fears
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages ended lower on Friday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND)) ending the week in the red as the AI trade came under pressure with Broadcom ((AVGO)) leading the decline.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed -1.7%, while the S&P 500 (SP500) finished -1.1%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI), which had earlier reached new highs, retreated to end -0.5%.

Week-to-date, the S&P 500 ((SP500)) lost -0.6%, and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND) shed -1.6%, while the Dow ((DJI)) gained +1%.

Chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) -11.6% warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders.

“This week the market had a lot to think about. Earnings from Broadcom ((AVGO)), perhaps the key bellwether AI name now, and the small matter of the price of money and the supply thereof, courtesy of Chairman Powell,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

“FOMC was plain bullish in our view – cheaper money and more liquidity; and with AVGO printing sustained high growth, high margins and bullish outlook, we have no reason to be bearish into year-end, panicans notwithstanding. Higher,” King added.

Also, among the Nasdaq’s ((COMP:IND)) top decliners are Micron Technology (MU), Applovin (APP), Palantir (PLTR), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), ending -6.7%, -6.5%, -2.2%, and -4.9%, respectively.

On the economic side, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waiting on more data before lowering interest rates would have been more prudent. Also, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she would prefer a “slightly more” restrictive monetary policy.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.20%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 1 basis point lower to 3.54%.

data.title: Corning slides after six-session rally
data.description:
Corning Incorporated (GLW) shares fell 7.96% to close at $88.83 on Friday, ending a six-session winning streak amid broader market softness.

The stock had surged about 12.3% between December 4 and December 11, far outpacing the S&P 500’s 0.64% rise over the same period.

The company last reported in late October, posting better-than-expected Q3 results with sales and earnings rising solidly. Executives said demand for its GenAI and solar products remained strong and projected another quarter of growth, while also expecting to reach a 20% operating margin earlier than planned.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, Corning is rated with a Strong Buy, with grades of A in momentum and revisions, B+ in terms of both growth and profitability, while a D in valuation.

However, SA analysts suggest holding the stock.

Analyst Daniel Jones said Corning is showing strong momentum in its core businesses and benefiting from AI and solar demand, the stock’s valuation remains a concern. He added that “shares remain expensive relative to peers, presenting valuation risks for value-oriented investors.”

Turning to the Wall Street, analysts are largely bullish, with 11 out of 14 analysts rating the stock with a Buy or higher, two rate it with a Hold and only one of them suggest a strong sell.

The stock remains up 98.21% year-to-date and has inched 0.47% higher over the past month.

data.title: Tether proposes to buy Exor's majority stake in Juventus Football Club
data.description:
Tether, the company behind USDT (USDT-USD), the world's largest stablecoin, has proposed to buy Exor's entire stake in Juventus Football Club (JVTSF) in a binding all-cash deal, it said on Friday.

Tether already owns a portion of Juventus (JVTSF), having boosted its stake in April to over 10.1%. Exor's shareholding represents 65.4% of Juventus’ (JVTSF) issued share capital. 

Upon completion of the planned deal, Tether intends to proceed with a public tender offer for the remaining shares at the same price per share, fully funded with its own capital, it said in a statement.

Should the transaction be completed, Tether said it will invest 1B euros ($1.17B) "in the support and development of the club."

data.title: Kevin Warsh 'would make a great' Fed chief, JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon says
data.description:
Former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh "would make a great" leader of the U.S. central bank, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon said during a recent event, according to a media report on Friday.

Warsh was previously considered for Fed chair during President Donald Trump's first term.

National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, who is still widely seen as the top candidate to succeed current Fed chief Jerome Powell in May 2026, would most likely reduce interest rates in the near term, aligning with Trump's view, Dimon said at the private event, Barron's reported. 

The NEC head urged the Fed last week to keep lowering rates, correctly predicting a third consecutive quarter-point reduction at this week's policy meeting.

data.title: Wealthfront stock opens flat in Nasdaq debut, picks pace in later part of day
data.description:
Financial platform Wealthfront (WLTH) opened its first day of trading on Nasdaq at $14, unchanged from its initial public offering price.

The Palo Alto, California-based fintech had sold 34.62M shares at $14 per share, raising ~$486M in its IPO.

Share price movement picked up pace in the later part of the trading day, with the stock trading 3.07% higher at $14.43 at 3.20 PM ET.

Comparatively, the Swedish buy now, pay later financing firm Klarna (KLAR) saw a strong NYSE debut, opening at a 30% premium to the $40 IPO price.

Similarly, Chime Financial (CHYM) stock had surged 59% from the IPO price in the opening trade, opening at $43 per share.

data.title: Ascendis Pharma gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Ascendis Pharma (ASND) ADRs quickly rose 8% amid some takeover speculation.
  • There's speculation that the Danish pharmaceutical firm may have attracted takeover interest. It's not clear who is interested in the company. 
  • Ascendis (ASND) has a market cap of $11.8 billion.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Economic data flurry, Micron earnings, and triple-witching day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - December 15

  • Volatility Watch - Bloom Energy (BE) and Hut 8 Corp. (HUT) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include CapsoVision (CV), Magnitude International (MAGH), and Photronics (PLAB). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include American Bitcoin (ABTC), Lakeland Industries (LAKE), and Rezolute (RZLT).
  • IPO watch - Medline (MDLN) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading during the week. The IPO lock-up period expires on certain blocks of shares of Slide Insurance (SLDE) and Enigmatig (EGG). The post-IPO quiet period ends on Central BanCo (CBC) and Gloo Holdings (GLOO) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Devon Energy (DVN), FedEx (FDX), and Disney (DIS).
  • All day - The NYC Summit 2025 is aimed at the semiconductor and related technology companies. The event will include a round-robin format of meetings between management teams and institutional investors/analysts. ACM Research (ACMR), Advanced Energy Industries (AEIS), and FormFactor (FORM) are some of the notable companies participating.
  • 10:30 a.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams participates in an economic growth discussion before an event organized by the New Jersey Bankers Association.

 

Tuesday - December 16

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Lennar (LEN) and Duluth Holdings (DLTH).
  • All day - The Northland Virtual Growth Conference will include participation from SunPower (SPWR), Asure Software (ASUR), and Calix (CALX).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (AJG) Investor Meeting, Vital Farms' (VITL) Investor Day, Cisco Systems' (CSCO) annual meeting, Neurocrine Biosciences' (NBIX) R&D Day, and Affirm Holdings' (AFRM) Investor Meeting.
  • All day - The go-shop period on Clayton Dubilier & Rice's offer to acquire Sealed Air (SEE) ends.
  • 8:00 a.m. Pfizer (PFE) is holding a conference call with investment analysts to provide its full-year 2026 financial guidance.
  • 8:30 a.m. The October and November jobs reports will be released. Economists expect a soft tally of ~50K job additions in November and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.4%.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau is scheduled to release the October Retail Sales report.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Council of Institutional Investors is holding a webinar to discuss "Tesla, Texas Corporate Law, and Investors." The topic could be intriguing for investors, as the impact of Texas business law on corporate governance and long-term shareholder value is a new wild card in the mix.

 

Wednesday - December 17

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Micron (MU), Jabil (JBL), General Mills (GIS), and Spire (SPIR).
  • Fedspeak - New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give opening remarks before the 2025 FX Market Structure Conference. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in a discussion on the economic outlook before the Gwinnett County Chamber of Commerce.
  • All day - Phillips Edison & Company (PECO) will hold a business update call.

 

Thursday - December 18

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Accenture (ACN), Nike (NKE), FedEx (FDX), CarMax (KMX), and Darden Restaurants (DRI). Options trading implies a double-digit move for BlackBerry (BB) and Scholastic (SCHL) after they report.
  • 7:45 a.m. The European Central Bank will release its policy statement.
  • 8:30 a.m. European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will hold a press conference.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for November will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. October components will be folded into the report.

 

Friday - December 19

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Carnival (CCL) and Conagra Brands (CAG).
  • All day - The stock market could see extra volatility with triple witching day featuring the simultaneous expiration of stock options, stock index futures, and stock index options contracts.
  • All day - WTI crude January futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • All day - Shareholders with STAAR Surgical (STAA) will vote on the buyout offer from Alcon (ALC). STAAR Surgical trades well below the $30.75 deal price.
  • All day - Disney (DIS) will release Avatar: Fire and Ash across theaters in the U.S. Box office expectations are for an opening weekend tally of over $100 million, which could be a late-year positive for AMC Entertainment (AMC), Cinemark Holdings (CNK), Marcus Corporation (MCS), and IMAX (IMAX).

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this report.

data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia's H200 chips, Coinbase eyes prediction markets
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • Nvidia (NVDA) is reportedly eyeing boosting production capacity for its H200 AI chips due to strong demand for the product in China.

Leading Chinese tech companies such as Alibaba (BABA) and ByteDance (BDNCE) have already reached out to Nvidia about buying the chips and are expected to place large orders. The Chinese government, however, has not yet decided whether it will allow the chips to be shipped to China, Reuters reported, citing sources close to the matter.

Reuters noted that Nvidia has limited quantities of H200 chips in production as it has been focusing on its new Blackwell and Rubin chips.

The Trump administration greenlighted sales of H200 chips to China earlier this week, ending an export ban that had been implemented over national security concerns. In return, Nvidia is expected to give the U.S. government 25% of the proceeds generated by sales of the chips.

  • Coinbase (COIN) is planning to launch products for prediction markets and tokenized equities.

The company will unveil the products on Dec. 17. Its tokenized equities will be launched in-house and not through a partner, according to Bloomberg, which cited a source close to the matter.

Bloomberg noted that Robinhood (HOOD) began offering Kalshi prediction market products earlier this year, while both Robinhood and Kraken (KRAKEN) offer tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs outside the U.S. Crypto.com and Trump Media (DJT) have also partnered to offer prediction market products.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb upgraded at Guggenheim on 2026 pipeline catalysts
data.description:
• Guggenheim upgraded Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) to buy from neutral saying that results for several pipeline candidates by the end of 2026 could lead to upside. 
  • The firm set a price target of $62 (21% upside based on Dec. 11 close). 
  • Analyst Seamus Fernandez highlighted two assets in particular: the oral Factor XIa inhibitor milvexian for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation and secondary stroke prevention; and admilparant, an LPA1 antagonist, for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and progressive pulmonary fibrosis.
  • In 2026, phase 3 results for milvexian in both indications are expected, as well as for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis for admilparant.
  • Fernandez noted that positive trial results for Bayer's (BAYZF)(BAYRY) own Factor XIa inhibitor, asundexian, bode well for milvexian. As a result, he is boosting his 2033 global sales estimate for milvexian to $6B from $2.2B.
  • He sees 2033 sales of admilparant of $2.5B, up from negligible prior.
  • Fernandez also sees Bristol benefitting from growth in royalties for pulmonary arterial hypertension treatment Winrevair (sotatercept), which is marketed by Merck (MRK).

data.title: Thinking Machines announces general availability of API
data.description:
• Thinking Machines, the artificial intelligence startup founded by former OpenAI (OPENAI) CTO Mira Murati, announced the general availability of its application programming interface on Friday.
  • Known as Tinker, the API supports models from a wide variety of companies, including Meta's (META) Llama, Alibaba's (BABA) Qwen, OpenAI's GPT-OSS and DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) V3.1.
  • Pricing for the API varies by model, according to the company's website.
  • Separately, on Friday, it added support for Moonshot's Kimi-2-Thinking model. “With a trillion parameters, Kimi K2 is the largest model in our lineup so far. It is built for long chains of reasoning and tool use,” Thinking Machines wrote on its website.
  • In July, Thinking Machines raised $2B in a funding round that valued it at $10B. Andreessen Horowitz, also known as a16z, led the funding round, which included participation by Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), ServiceNow (NOW), Cisco Systems (CSCO) and others.
